  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Pump is pumping toward the expansion tank connection, violating the point-of-no-pressure-change principle.",
    "Sub-atmospheric pressure conditions may occur on the suction side of the pump, leading to air ingress and cavitation."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Isolate the hydronic loop and drain the affected piping section.",
    "Reconfigure the piping layout to locate the expansion tank connection on the suction side of the pump.",
    "Purge air from the system, refill, and verify stable operating pressures."
  ],
  "rationale": "In hydronic systems, the expansion tank connection is the point of no pressure change (PONPC). Pumping away from this point increases system pressure during pump operation. Pumping toward this point causes a pressure drop on the suction side of the pump, which can drop system pressure below atmospheric, drawing air in through automatic air vents and causing pump cavitation, noise, and premature component degradation.",
  "references": [
    "ASHRAE HVAC Systems and Equipment Handbook - Hydronic Heating and Cooling System Design",
    "Manufacturer Installation Manuals for Hydronic Expansion Tanks and Pumps"
  ]
}
